claim: For all integers n >= 1, the exponent of 2 in (2n)! is at least n.
predicate: n < 1 or vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) >= n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the exponent of 2 in (2n)! is at least the exponent of 2 in n!
predicate: n < 1 or vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) >= vp(math.factorial(n), 2)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2, then the exponent of 2 in (2n)! is exactly n.
predicate: n < 1 or (n & (n-1)!= 0) or vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) == n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is composite, then the exponent of 2 in (2n)! is greater than the exponent of 2 in n!
predicate: n < 1 or is_prime(n) or vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) > vp(math.factorial(n), 2)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2 and composite, then the exponent of 2 in (2n)! is greater than n.
predicate: n < 1 or (n & (n-1)!= 0) or is_prime(n) or vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) > n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the exponent of 3 in (2n)! is at least n/2.
predicate: n < 1 or vp(math.factorial(2*n), 3) >= n // 2

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a multiple of 3, then the exponent of 3 in (2n)! is at least n.
predicate: n < 1 or (n % 3!= 0) or vp(math.factorial(2*n), 3) >= n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the exponent of 2 in (2n)! is greater than or equal to the exponent of 3 in (2n)!
predicate: n < 1 or vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) >= vp(math.factorial(2*n), 3)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2 and a multiple of 3, then the exponent of 2 in (2n)! is greater than n and the exponent of 3 in (2n)! is at least n.
predicate: n < 1 or (n & (n-1)!= 0) or (n % 3!= 0) or (vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) > n and vp(math.factorial(2*n), 3) >= n)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the exponent of 2 in (2n)! plus the exponent of 3 in (2n)! is at least 3n/2.
predicate: n < 1 or vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) + vp(math.factorial(2*n), 3) >= 3 * n // 2
